item.title: Donald Trump’s Superficiality Is Bone Deep
item.description: For the shallowest man ever to occupy the presidency, surface appeal is a guiding principle.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: Another Chance for Trump to Cash Out
item.description: The president’s battle over presidential records is more serious than it looks.
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: The Left Needs to Rediscover Its Patriotism
item.description: A left that rejects a hopeful, empathetic love of the United States can never win the country to its side.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kazin

item.title: AI Has Ruined the Job Market
item.description: Maybe flawed people were better than brute algorithms.
item.dc:creator: Annie Lowrey

item.title: Trump’s Strange Choice for Director of National Intelligence
item.description: The president’s selection of Bill Pulte is both baffling and predictable.
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: Democrats Must Learn to Talk Sports
item.description: Politicians need as much attention as possible, as frequently as possible, while seeming as relatable as possible. A cheat code exists to hit all three objectives.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Frum

item.title: All These Defeats Are Ruining Trump’s Birthday
item.description: The end of Trump’s “anti-weaponization” fund is the latest in a series of setbacks.
item.dc:creator: Idrees Kahloon

item.title: How to Silence the Federal Workforce
item.description: A proposal to push would-be whistleblowers to sign nondisclosure agreements is part of a bigger effort to hide government secrets.
item.dc:creator: Quinta Jurecic

item.title: The Spanish Exception
item.description: The country’s leaders avoided a populist backlash by engineering an economic boom. Now the boom is creating problems of its own.
item.dc:creator: Rogé Karma

item.title: Use It or Lose It
item.description: Freedom of speech, and of the press, can be guaranteed only if Americans exercise their rights.
item.dc:creator: Adrienne LaFrance
